items.title: USDA lists approved SNAP waivers restricting purchases like soda and candy in multiple states
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP waiver tracker shows multiple states with approved requests to restrict SNAP purchases of items such as soda/soft drinks and candy. The page lists target implementation dates ranging from 2026-01-01 (e.g., Indiana, Iowa, Nebraska, Utah, West Virginia) to later dates such as 2026-10-30 (Colorado) and 2028-02-01 (Nevada).

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS issued guidance to prepare states to implement statutory changes that limit the ability to claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens. CMS says states must implement the change starting 2026-10-01, while federal matching funds remain available for emergency medical conditions and the changes do not apply to lawfully residing children and pregnant people covered under the ICHIA option.

items.title: KFF details upcoming Medicaid changes, including work requirements and 6-month renewals starting in 2027
items.summary: KFF reports that federal policy changes will require states to implement work/community engagement requirements for ACA expansion adults starting in 2027-01 and to move expansion adults to 6-month renewal periods starting in 2027-01. The analysis also highlights an immigrant eligibility restriction date of 2026-10-01 and administrative requirements like using the USPS National Change of Address database beginning 2027-01-01.

items.title: HUD issues FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding notice with set-aside application deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 lays out FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding and key deadlines, including HAP renewal funding of $34.557B and administrative fees of $2.835935B. The notice also lists a $400M HAP set-aside and deadlines such as 2026-06-26 (multiple set-aside categories), 2026-07-10 (blended-rate and higher admin fee requests), and 2026-12-18 (disaster set-aside requests).
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: SSA announces 2.8% COLA for 2026 and updated SSI federal payment standards
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment. It also lists the SSI federal payment standard for 2026 as $994 per month for an individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit amounts for fruits and vegetables
items.summary: USDA’s WIC policy memo lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo states these amounts apply for fiscal year 2026 (2025-10-01 through 2026-09-30).

items.title: FAFSA federal deadline: submit 2025–26 FAFSA by June 30, 2026 (and 2026–27 by June 30, 2027)
items.summary: USA.gov states the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2025–26 school year is 2026-06-30 and for the 2026–27 school year is 2027-06-30. The page notes that states and colleges also use FAFSA information to award aid, so earlier submission may matter for non-federal deadlines.

items.title: IRS posts 2025 Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) limits and maximum credits
items.summary: IRS EITC tables for tax year 2025 show maximum credit amounts of $649 (no qualifying children), $4,328 (one child), $7,152 (two children), and $8,046 (three or more children). The tables also list maximum AGI limits up to $61,555 (single/HOH) or $68,675 (married filing jointly) for families with 3 children, and an investment income limit of $11,950.

items.title: IRS Schedule 8812 instructions list 2025 Child Tax Credit and Additional Child Tax Credit amounts
items.summary: IRS instructions for Schedule 8812 (Form 1040) state the maximum Child Tax Credit for each qualifying child increased to $2,200 for tax year 2025, and the maximum Additional Child Tax Credit is $1,700 per qualifying child. The instructions also list phaseout thresholds starting above $400,000 modified AGI for married filing jointly and $200,000 for all other filing statuses.
